title: Add bounded in-memory session retention controls

requirements.acceptance: Setting a documented env var to a positive integer causes the plugin to retain no more than that many inactive sessions after pruning.
Setting a documented env var to a positive age removes sessions older than that age while preserving sessions updated within the threshold.
With no retention env vars set, existing tests and public helper behavior remain backward-compatible.
get_tps_stats() returns the existing zero-value structure for pruned sessions and never recreates pruned sessions merely by reading stats.
No external packages, background threads, REST servers, schedulers, or process-wide timers are introduced.
get_observability_contract() or related diagnostics expose enabled/disabled state, env var names, and active numeric limits without raw session identifiers.
Retention diagnostics do not expose raw session IDs, model names, provider names, salts, or other sensitive identifiers.
Tests exercise pruning while reads/writes occur and confirm no crashes or inconsistent public return shapes.

success_criteria: Retention is disabled by default and existing TPS/session behavior remains compatible.
Enabling HERMES_TPS_MAX_SESSIONS prunes the in-memory session dictionary to the configured bound.
Enabling HERMES_TPS_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS prunes stale sessions without sleeping in real time.
Pruned sessions return the existing zero-value stats shape.
Retention policy is documented in the observability contract or diagnostics without exposing sensitive identifiers.
No external dependencies, background threads, package manager commands, or Hermes core changes are required.
out_of_scope: SQLite persistence, historical exports, or persisted-row retention.
REST, WebSocket, Prometheus, or dashboard APIs.
Sampling of call events or SQLite write amplification controls covered by her-6od.
Batch session stats endpoint work covered by her-feat-batch-session-stats-ojy.
